results.possibleIdentity: Joshua Friedman is an electrical engineer and dentist who was active in New York City during the late 1960s and 1970s. During this period, he was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization promoting collaborations between artists and engineers. Specifically, he collaborated with Charles Tafra on a project for the E.A.T. exhibition 'Some More Beginnings,' held at the Brooklyn Museum in 1968. Friedman lived at 350 East 30th Street in New York (ZIP code 10016), an address he used for both his 1972 patent filings and his registration as a licensed radio amateur (call sign WB2EAM). Professionally, he served as an Assistant Research Scientist at the NYU College of Dentistry, where he applied his electrical engineering background to develop dental medical devices. He is recognized as a pioneer in light-curing technology and dental instrumentation, having founded the Demetron Research Corporation and AdDent, Inc. His technical contributions include the invention of a compact battery-operated dental pulp tester, for which he received a U.S. patent in 1973.
